public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as night-lantern inspector.
appearance: Practical layers, inspection tools, and a habit of carrying evidence or notes keep inquiry visibly close at hand.
manner: Asks more than they answer, preserving doubt until the evidence can bear weight. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a night-lantern inspector still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of night-lantern inspector in and around Harvest Circle, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Harvest Circle workable through the obligations of night-lantern inspector while managing the immediate pressure of spoilage in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must keep evidence and interpretation aligned while rival interests try to redirect the inquiry. Around Harvest Circle, that usually means carrying spoilage, price pressure, favor feuds without letting the role of night-lantern inspector visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Garren Ashmason needs discreet assistance following contradictory evidence, frightened witnesses, or a lead too politically awkward to pursue openly in Harvest Circle.
Working with Garren Ashmason can place the party inside an active inquiry where every answer creates new exposure.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Questioning silence, inspection habits, and careful note-taking make Garren Ashmason look like inquiry before accusation. In Harvest Circle, this lands inside a public field already shaped by harvest stewards, quota clerks.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Circle: inquiry rooms, witness routes, guarded evidence sites, or contested scenes
who_seeks_them_out: those needing a problem solved quietly

first_impression: Garren Ashmason presents as Asks more than they answer, preserving doubt until the evidence can bear weight. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a night-lantern inspector still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Night-Lantern Inspector, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Garren Ashmason refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Night-Lantern Inspector too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Garren Ashmason can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Garren Ashmason adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Garren Ashmason is Known locally through day-to-day work as night-lantern inspector. and rarely acts without a second motive.
